nodes.label: DevSwarm
nodes.summary: Prompt-to-full-stack-app generator using a swarm of specialized open-weight models, review-gated by a critic and fully traced into SigNoz.

nodes.label: SigNoz
nodes.summary: Self-hosted observability platform that receives all swarm traces, metrics and logs and powers dashboards, alerts and the Swarm Doctor.

nodes.label: OpenTelemetry
nodes.summary: Instrumentation standard used across the swarm: one root span per generation, child spans per agent and per LLM call.

nodes.label: Hugging Face Inference Providers
nodes.summary: Serving layer for every open-weight model in the swarm; requires HF_TOKEN and avoids closed frontier APIs entirely.

nodes.label: Agents of SigNoz hackathon
nodes.summary: WeMakeDevs hackathon (July 20-26, 2026) DevSwarm is built for, entered on Track 1: AI & Agent Observability.

nodes.label: Planner agent
nodes.summary: Turns the user prompt into a strict JSON build plan (name, entities, api, pages) whose api array is law for all downstream agents.

nodes.label: Frontend agent
nodes.summary: Generates one self-contained index.html (inline CSS/JS, no frameworks) that calls the contract endpoints exactly, with a localStorage fallback.

nodes.label: Backend agent
nodes.summary: Generates a single ESM Express server.js with an in-memory store, implementing the plan's api array verbatim with input validation.

nodes.label: Critic agent
nodes.summary: Independent review agent that checks contract conformance, security and runtime bugs on scoped checklists and returns a pass/fail JSON verdict.

nodes.label: Swarm Doctor
nodes.summary: SRE meta-agent that consumes SigNoz data (traces, metrics, alerts) to diagnose swarm health in natural language and execute self-healing fallback promotions.

nodes.label: Testing/QA agent
nodes.summary: Planned agent that generates and runs tests against the live preview and routes failures back to the owning codegen agent; cut from the hackathon MVP.

nodes.label: Documentation agent
nodes.summary: Planned agent that generates README, setup and API docs from the plan plus the actual generated code; cut from the hackathon MVP.

nodes.label: Deployment/DevOps agent
nodes.summary: Planned agent for Dockerfiles, CI/CD and IaC that also injects OTel bootstrapping into generated apps; a fixed React+Express template stands in for it in the MVP.

nodes.label: Orchestrator
nodes.summary: src/orchestrator.js: runs the flow of plan, parallel codegen, critic gate and the max-2 regeneration loop, writing output to generated/<id>/.

nodes.label: LLM client (llm.js)
nodes.summary: src/llm.js: HF router client handling fallback promotion on primary failure, JSON/code extraction and GenAI semconv span attributes.

nodes.label: Model routing table (models.js)
nodes.summary: src/models.js: role-to-model routing table plus runtime promoted overrides, treated as config rather than a hardcoded constant.

nodes.label: Telemetry setup (telemetry.js)
nodes.summary: src/telemetry.js: OTel setup producing one root span per generation with child spans per agent and per LLM call.

nodes.label: DevSwarm server (server.js)
nodes.summary: src/server.js: Express API on port 4100 exposing /api/generate plus static preview of generated apps and the UI.

nodes.label: Mission Control UI
nodes.summary: ui/app.html: the judge-facing single-screen surface with Swarm Graph, Trace River and Vitals Rail, driven entirely by live telemetry events.

nodes.label: Landing page
nodes.summary: ui/index.html: dark marketing site for DevSwarm with an animated trace hero, model strip, how-it-works grid and observability stat cards.

nodes.label: Swarm Graph
nodes.summary: Mission Control's hero zone: a live node graph of the four agents with role-colored pulses, flowing edges and critic flag chips.

nodes.label: Trace River
nodes.summary: Mission Control's bottom zone: a live horizontal span waterfall of the current generation, color-coded by role, deep-linking to SigNoz.

nodes.label: Vitals Rail
nodes.summary: Mission Control's right rail: live token odometer, critic catch count, per-role model chips with FALLBACK badges, Swarm Doctor card and alert feed.

nodes.label: SSE event stream (/api/events)
nodes.summary: Server-sent event feed of llm_start/llm_end, fallback, critic_catch, stage, doctor_diagnosis, done and error events consumed by Mission Control.

nodes.label: Build plan / API contract
nodes.summary: Planner-owned strict JSON plan (entities, api, pages) that is the single source of truth binding frontend and backend against contract drift.

nodes.label: Regeneration loop
nodes.summary: Critic issues route back only to the flagged agent(s) as feedback, for at most 2 regenerations, then the app ships with the review attached.

nodes.label: Fallback promotion
nodes.summary: On primary model failure the role is switched to its fallback model, recorded as an explicit span event and surfaced as a FALLBACK badge.

nodes.label: GenAI semantic conventions
nodes.summary: Every span on the LLM path must carry gen_ai.* semconv attributes plus devswarm.role; untraced work defeats the project thesis.

nodes.label: Born-observable generated apps
nodes.summary: Every generated app ships already instrumented with OTel and gets an auto-provisioned SigNoz dashboard alongside its README.

nodes.label: otel-swarm instrumentation library
nodes.summary: Planned reusable open-source OTel library for any multi-agent LLM system, GenAI semconv-compliant, shipped with importable SigNoz dashboard JSON.

nodes.label: Transparency panel
nodes.summary: User-facing panel showing which model handled which part and what the critic flagged, backed by the same SigNoz trace data as ops views.

nodes.label: Live preview sandbox
nodes.summary: Isolated ephemeral runtime that actually runs generated apps; per-session network and filesystem isolation is a hard security requirement.

nodes.label: Sandbox isolation requirement
nodes.summary: Every generated app is treated as hostile until reviewed: sandboxes must be network- and filesystem-isolated per session as a launch requirement.

nodes.label: Qwen3.6-Plus
nodes.summary: Apache-2.0 1M-context model chosen as the planner's primary for tool-calling reliability and long-context task decomposition.

nodes.label: GLM-5.2
nodes.summary: Zhipu MoE MIT-licensed 1M-context model, primary for frontend codegen as leader of open-model agentic web-dev benchmarks.

nodes.label: Qwen3-Coder-480B
nodes.summary: Apache-2.0 1M-context coder model, primary for backend codegen (and planned testing agent) for balanced correctness on SWE-bench-style tasks.

nodes.label: DeepSeek-V4
nodes.summary: MIT-licensed thinking-mode model, primary for the critic because of its reasoning and self-verification profile.

nodes.label: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
nodes.summary: Fallback model for the planner role.

nodes.label: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
nodes.summary: Cheaper fallback model for the backend (and planned testing) roles.

nodes.label: Kimi K2.6
nodes.summary: Fallback for the frontend role (especially screenshot/Figma-to-code) and a second-opinion critic option in thinking mode.

nodes.label: Kimi K2.7 Code
nodes.summary: Model shown in the landing page's swarm strip as one of the served backend-family models.

nodes.label: Generation Overview dashboard
nodes.summary: SigNoz dashboard with span waterfall KPIs, generation success rate and time-to-app.

nodes.label: LLM Economics dashboard
nodes.summary: SigNoz dashboard of tokens and cost per role, model and provider, including fallback events.

nodes.label: Review-Gate Funnel dashboard
nodes.summary: SigNoz dashboard of critic catches by severity, regeneration loops and catch rate over time.

nodes.label: Fallback-usage spike alert
nodes.summary: SigNoz alert rule that fires when fallback-model usage spikes, notifying the swarm-doctor webhook channel.

nodes.label: Critic catch-rate flatline alert
nodes.summary: SigNoz alert rule that fires when the critic catch rate drops to zero, signalling a silently broken critic.

nodes.label: Swarm Doctor webhook channel
nodes.summary: SigNoz webhook notification channel pointing at http://host.docker.internal:4100/api/doctor/webhook so alerts wake Swarm Doctor automatically.

nodes.label: DevSwarm observability pack
nodes.summary: observability/ folder bundling the Foundry casting, three importable dashboards and two v2alpha1 alert rules, validated against live swarm data on 2026-07-21.

nodes.label: Foundry casting (casting.yaml)
nodes.summary: v1alpha1 Installation manifest that self-hosts SigNoz with the compose flavor in docker mode.

nodes.label: SigNoz Docker Compose stack
nodes.summary: compose.yaml defining the signoz network and the ingester, server, ClickHouse, Keeper, Postgres, migrator and user-scripts services.

nodes.label: SigNoz ingester (OTel collector)
nodes.summary: signoz-otel-collector service receiving OTLP on 4317 (gRPC) and 4318 (HTTP) and exporting traces, logs, metrics, meter and metadata to ClickHouse.

nodes.label: SigNoz server
nodes.summary: signoz/signoz container on port 8080 serving the SigNoz UI and API, backed by Postgres for app state and ClickHouse for telemetry.

nodes.label: ClickHouse telemetry store
nodes.summary: clickhouse-server 25.12.5 storing signoz_traces, signoz_logs, signoz_metrics, signoz_meter and signoz_metadata databases on port 9000.

nodes.label: ClickHouse Keeper
nodes.summary: clickhouse-keeper 25.12.5 providing Raft coordination on port 9181; replicas can sit readonly after a cold restart until it reconnects.

nodes.label: Postgres metastore
nodes.summary: postgres:16 container holding the SigNoz sqlstore (users, dashboards, rules) at signoz-metastore-postgres-0.

nodes.label: Telemetrystore migrator
nodes.summary: One-shot signoz-otel-collector container that bootstraps and runs sync/async schema migrations against ClickHouse.

nodes.label: ClickHouse user-scripts init
nodes.summary: Init container that downloads the histogram-quantile binary from SigNoz releases into ClickHouse's user_scripts volume.

nodes.label: histogramQuantile UDF
nodes.summary: Executable ClickHouse user-defined function (buckets, counts, quantile to Float64) declared in functions.yaml and backed by the fetched binary.

nodes.label: OpAMP management channel
nodes.summary: The ingester's manager config points at ws://signoz-signoz-0:4320/v1/opamp so the SigNoz server remotely manages collector configuration.

nodes.label: signozspanmetrics processor
nodes.summary: Collector processor deriving delta-temporality latency histograms and span metrics from the traces pipeline into ClickHouse metrics.

nodes.label: Review catch rate
nodes.summary: Percentage of generations where the critic caught and fixed a real bug or security issue before user-facing output; the core value-prop metric.

nodes.label: Cost per generation
nodes.summary: Per-generation and per-role token/cost breakdown versus closed-model tools; the cheaper-than-closed-models thesis as a live dashboard.

nodes.label: Generation success rate
nodes.summary: Percentage of prompts producing a working deployable app, pre-review versus after review-gate fixes.

nodes.label: Time to working app
nodes.summary: Elapsed time from prompt to deployed working app, computed live from SigNoz traces.

nodes.label: Mission Control design language
nodes.summary: design.md: the-observability-data-is-the-interface design system with role color tokens, telemetry-driven motion rules and flight-ops voice.

nodes.label: Generated-app design system
nodes.summary: swarm/design.md: rules every generated app follows (responsive, accessible, no banned default palettes, one signature element), fed verbatim to the frontend agent.

nodes.label: Swarm role contracts
nodes.summary: swarm/agents.md: what each agent receives, must produce and is judged on; the contract is changed here before the prompt.

nodes.label: DevSwarm PRD
nodes.summary: PRD.md: product requirements including the swarm architecture, SigNoz observability layer, hackathon strategy and success metrics.

nodes.label: DevSwarm AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Working notes for the repo: run instructions, code map, conventions and paid-for gotchas like thinking-model token budgets and provider response shapes.

nodes.label: 3-minute demo script
nodes.summary: Scripted continuous take: dispatch a prompt, show a critic catch, kill a model endpoint to trigger Swarm Doctor self-healing, open the born-observable app, close on the LLM Economics dashboard.

nodes.label: Scoped critic checks rule
nodes.summary: Critic prompts are scoped verifiable checklists (contract, security, runtime bugs), never open-ended code-quality judgment, to avoid critic hallucination.

nodes.label: No em/en dash rule
nodes.summary: Em and en dashes are banned everywhere: code comments, commit messages, docs, UI copy and generated text.
